title: Hibbertia angustifolia
intro: Hibbertia angustifolia, also known as Guinea flower, is a small shrub native to Australia, belonging to the family Dilleniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Hibbertia angustifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hibbertia angustifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
taxonomy: Hibbertia angustifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. This plant is native to Australia and is part of the genus Hibbertia. The family Dilleniaceae is a small group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the bellows flowers. Hibbertia angustifolia is a member of the order Dilleniales and the clade angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. It is one of the over 200 species found within the Hibbertia genus, characterized by its small, white flowers and narrow leaves.
